themes.topic: AI market downturn analysis and bull case
themes.summary: The current AI market downturn is attributed to Broadcom's guidance, hawkish Fed sentiment, and 'AI bubble' commentary, but underlying physical demand for wafers, HBM, CoWoS, and optics remains strong and supply-constrained. Hyperscaler capex is projected to rise, and the shift to inference-led demand suggests reliable recurring revenue, indicating the sell-off is a reset rather than a fundamental demand collapse.

themes.topic: Samsung Foundry's resurgence and capacity allocation
themes.summary: Samsung Foundry is experiencing a strong comeback, with 4nm capacity sold out through next year and 8nm processes at full utilization, leading to selective order taking for large customers and potential price hikes of 15-20%.

mentions.text:
Samsung Foundry has really made a full comeback. It feels surreal seeing how much things have changed.

- Samsung Foundry is seeing so many customers pile in that Samsung is now screening customers, favoring large ones.

- Samsung's 4nm is already sold out through next year's cap

No surprises here, I have been calling that Samsung Foundry will make a full comeback for some time now (and that they will execute on HBM4e)

Price action will eventually follow fundamentals - this dip is a gift!

themes.topic: Coherent (COHR) secures 6-inch InP wafer supply from AXT (AXTI)
themes.summary: Coherent signed a 3-year Master Development and Supply Agreement with AXT-Tongmei for 6-inch indium phosphide (InP) wafer substrates, involving a $22.3M prepayment to secure capacity expansion at AXT's Beijing facility. This de-risks Coherent's AI optical ramp by locking in a critical upstream supply.

themes.topic: Persistent GPU supply tightness despite misleading spot market signals
themes.summary: Despite some reports of lower GPU spot prices, large-cloud and enterprise demand for H100 and B300 GPUs remains strong and supply-constrained, with AWS pricing firm and neocloud providers reporting demand significantly exceeding supply.

mentions.text:
Research|Korea's AI Investment Plan: Short-to-Medium Term Supply Tightness Cannot be Alleviated

Spot market signals may be misleading: Recent Ornn and Silicon Data datasets show lower GPU spot prices, especially for H100, but these sources mainly aggregate fragmented, smaller-sc

themes.topic: Custom AI chip proliferation and manufacturing bottlenecks
themes.summary: The proliferation of custom AI chips from hyperscalers like Meta and Amazon is highlighting persistent manufacturing bottlenecks in areas such as advanced packaging, substrates, and PCB materials, shifting the focus from chip design to the capacity required to build and package these custom silicon solutions.

mentions.text:
AI chip design is becoming more modular. AI chip manufacturing is not.

Recently we have seen:

- Meta / MediaTek: another hyperscaler custom-chip path
- Amazon / Alchip: ASIC design-service work keeps moving upstream
- Huawei / Korea: non-US AI stacks are still trying to export

themes.topic: MediaTek pursuing Meta AI accelerator deal and expanding custom ASIC business
themes.summary: MediaTek is actively pursuing an AI accelerator chip deal with Meta and is expanding its custom ASIC business, with Google as its primary client for chips like Zebrafish and Humufish, aiming for $10 billion in revenue.

themes.topic: Micron (MU) CEO on strong, long-term memory demand and supply tightness
themes.summary: Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra highlighted strong, secular long-term demand trends for memory driven by AI, projecting supply tightness beyond 2027, and discussed Micron's significant investments and strategic customer agreements to meet this demand.

themes.topic: Meta's (META) NeoCloud strategy and tiered AI compute infrastructure
themes.summary: Meta's NeoCloud initiative and consideration of external compute commercialization do not signal peak AI demand, but rather a shift to a tiered AI infrastructure where older GPUs are repurposed for inference while next-generation systems like GB300 remain scarce for frontier training.

mentions.text:
Research| $META : Building a NeoCloud and Continuing to Rent Capacity are not Contradictory

themes.topic: Underestimation of Chinese AI capex, ByteDance's $100B spending
themes.summary: Chinese AI capex is likely underestimated by focusing solely on big tech, with ByteDance alone projected to spend over $100B next year, and contributions from SOEs and telecom operators needing to be included.

mentions.text:
U will always get a wrong number if u try to use only Chinese big tech numbers to size AI CAPEX
First, drop the loser Baidu
Second, add the Chinese SOEs & the telecom operators
Also, ByteDance is spending over $100B next year

themes.topic: Tesla (TSLA) caps employee AI spending at $200/week
themes.summary: Tesla is reportedly implementing a $200 per week cap on AI spending for its employees, with exceptions for xAI beta products, after some software engineers were using thousands of dollars worth of AI tokens weekly.

mentions.text:

themes.topic: Nvidia's Nemotron development and post-training stack
themes.summary: Nvidia is actively developing its Nemotron post-training stack, having started serious efforts in Q4 last year and recently releasing Nemotron v4 Ultra, with expectations to match competitor models by Q4.

themes.topic: Ams Osram (AMS) pivots to AI photonics via MicroLED after non-optical segment sale
themes.summary: Ams Osram sold its non-optical segment to Infineon for €570M to de-risk its balance sheet and fund a pivot into AI photonics, specifically MicroLED technology, which could be a beneficiary if adopted in racks.

mentions.text:
Ams Osram selling their non-optical/sensor segment to Infineon for €570M is a pretty key catalyst.

For balance sheet de-risking (debt halving).

And pretty important to fuel their pivot into AI photonics (e.g. MicroLED) since names like Ayar Labs and Celestial AI all sit on hype

themes.topic: Marvell (MRVL) revenue growth driven by Google CXL business
themes.summary: Marvell is projected to see significant revenue growth, reaching $13.0B by 2028, primarily driven by its Google CXL-related business.

mentions.text: "GFHK: MRVL is expected to generate $2.8B / $13.0B in revenue in 2027 / 2028, respectively, driven by Google CXL-related business"
